exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Polar bears have black skin underneath their white-appearing fur. Each hair is actually a hollow, transparent tube that scatters light, making the bear look white. This coloring provides excellent camouflage against Arctic snow and ice, allowing polar bears to sneak up on seals, their primary prey.

questions.question: If polar bears lived in a forest instead of the Arctic, how might their survival be affected?
options: Their white appearance would make them visible to prey, reducing hunting success
Their fur would become solid white
Forests have more seals so they would thrive
questions.correctOption: Their white appearance would make them visible to prey, reducing hunting success

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: The Library of Alexandria, founded around 300 BC in Egypt, was the largest library of the ancient world. It aimed to collect a copy of every book ever written. Ships entering the harbor of Alexandria were required to surrender any scrolls they carried for copying. The library's destruction, which happened gradually over centuries, is considered one of history's greatest losses of knowledge.

questions.question: What does the policy of copying scrolls from ships reveal about how the library's founders viewed knowledge?
options: They believed collecting and preserving all knowledge was extremely important
They wanted to steal from travelers
They needed paper for other projects
They did not trust foreign writers
questions.correctOption: They believed collecting and preserving all knowledge was extremely important

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Bamboo is technically a grass, not a tree, yet some species can grow up to 91 centimeters in a single day. It is one of the strongest natural materials on Earth relative to its weight. In many Asian countries, bamboo is used for building scaffolding on skyscrapers because it is both lightweight and incredibly flexible.

questions.question: Why is flexibility an important quality for scaffolding on tall buildings?
options: Flexible materials can absorb wind and movement without breaking
Because workers prefer to bounce while working
Because flexible materials are always cheaper
Because skyscrapers are made entirely of bamboo
questions.correctOption: Flexible materials can absorb wind and movement without breaking

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Nina opened the closet and pulled out the violin case. A layer of dust coated the top. She unlatched it carefully and ran her fingers over the strings. They were out of tune and one was broken. She stared at it for a long time, then whispered, 'Okay. It's time to start again.'

questions.question: What can you infer about Nina's relationship with the violin?
acceptableAnswers: she used to play but stopped
she gave up violin and wants to start again
she quit playing a long time ago
she is returning to something she left behind
questions.sampleAnswer: Nina used to play the violin but stopped some time ago, as shown by the dust and broken string, and she has now decided to start playing again.

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: Every morning, Abuela braided Rosa's hair before school. She hummed the same song each time—a melody from a place Rosa had never been. 'What song is that?' Rosa asked one day. Abuela's hands paused. 'It's the song my mother sang to me when I was your age, back home.' She resumed braiding, humming a little softer.

questions.question: Why does Abuela hum softer after answering Rosa's question?
acceptableAnswers: she misses her home and her mother
thinking about the past moved her emotionally
questions.sampleAnswer: Abuela hums softer because talking about the song brought up emotional memories of her mother and the home she left behind.

exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the question.

data.passage: The two teams shook hands at the end of the game. Most players walked away quickly, but the goalkeeper from the losing team stayed behind. She found the striker who had scored the winning goal and said, 'That was an incredible shot. I couldn't have stopped it.' The striker looked surprised, then smiled. 'Thanks. Your saves in the first half were unreal.'

questions.question: What does this exchange reveal about both players?
questions.sampleAnswer: Both players show excellent sportsmanship by genuinely complimenting each other's abilities despite being on opposing sides.
